learnerValue: Distinguishes Hitler’s appointment as Chancellor from the subsequent establishment of dictatorship.
Tests retrieval of the complete specification sequence rather than only the Reichstag Fire and Enabling Act.
Reinforces the need to compare a Norman site’s features with earlier periods when judging representativeness.
Tests the central historic-environment method of connecting a specific place with associated events and developments.
Separates what site design reflects from the other required aspects of historic-environment study.
The three distractors represent distinct, source-grounded category confusions.
Builds accurate awareness of the historic environment’s course weighting and approximate guided-learning allocation.
